subject: FW: D Conference Coverage: May 27-29
body:
From: D6 Conference Team [mailto:register@dwsj.com] 
Sent: Monday, May 19, 2008 3:50 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: D Conference Coverage: May 27-29

 

Hello,

 

The sixth edition of The Wall Street Journal's D: All Things Digital
executive conference is fast approaching!  While the May 27 to 29 event
has already reached sold out status, we wanted to update you on the
coverage that will be available on our AllThingsD.com site because there
are a number of ways that you can experience some of the D6 action
online.

 

First, you may have noticed that the AllThingsD.com homepage
<http://www.allthingsd.com>  has a new look. We redesigned the page to
provide more news and headlines, updated throughout the day. A combined
blog section in the center column integrates posts from Kara Swisher and
John Paczkowski. The most recent columns by Walt Mossberg are also
available at the top right of the page. A new feature, "Tech Around the
Web," showcases feeds from popular sites like GigaOm, TechMeme, Digg and
paidContent. 

 

Throughout the D6 conference, we'll be live blogging and posting photos
and video of the sessions in our video section
<http://video.allthingsd.com/> . Be sure to stop by and check it out.
It's the next best thing to being there in person!

 

Also, as a special feature this year, you can submit a text or video
question for any of the D6 speakers right on the AllThingsD.com site.
It's your chance to ask whatever you like of speakers like Microsoft
chairman Bill Gates, Federal Communications Commission chairman Kevin
Martin, Activision CEO Robert Zotick and Facebook founder and CEO Mark
Zuckerberg. And, if your question is selected, executive producers Walt
Mossberg and Kara Swisher will ask it live on stage during D6.  The
deadline is this Friday, May 23rd, and submission details and
instructions are online at: http://allthingsd.com/d/ask-a-question/.

 

In addition to the luminaries mentioned above, we've added new speakers
to the D6 lineup in the last few weeks, including Jerry Yang and Sue
Decker of Yahoo, Sheryl Sandberg of Facebook and Rupert Murdoch of News
Corporation. The full speaker list is available at:
http://allthingsd.com/d/speakers/.

 

Best,
D6 Conference Team

***
You received this email because you previously signed up for updates
about The Wall Street Journal's D: All Things Digital conference. If you
do not wish to receive these types of notices in the future, please
reply and type "Remove" in the subject line. Or, send email to
lists@dwsj.com.
body_html:
From: D6 Conference Team [mailto:register@dwsj.com]
Sent: Monday, May 19, 2008 3:50 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: D Conference Coverage: May 27-29

 

Hello,

 

The sixth edition of The Wall Street Journal's D: All Things Digital executive conference is fast approaching!  While the May 27 to 29 event has already reached sold out status, we wanted to update you on the coverage that will be available on our AllThingsD.com site because there are a number of ways that you can experience some of the D6 action online.

 

First, you may have noticed that the AllThingsD.com homepage has a new look. We redesigned the page to provide more news and headlines, updated throughout the day. A combined blog section in the center column integrates posts from Kara Swisher and John Paczkowski. The most recent columns by Walt Mossberg are also available at the top right of the page. A new feature, "Tech Around the Web," showcases feeds from popular sites like GigaOm, TechMeme, Digg and paidContent.

 

Throughout the D6 conference, we'll be live blogging and posting photos and video of the sessions in our video section. Be sure to stop by and check it out. It's the next best thing to being there in person!

 

Also, as a special feature this year, you can submit a text or video question for any of the D6 speakers right on the AllThingsD.com site. It's your chance to ask whatever you like of speakers like Microsoft chairman Bill Gates, Federal Communications Commission chairman Kevin Martin, Activision CEO Robert Zotick and Facebook founder and CEO Mark Zuckerberg. And, if your question is selected, executive producers Walt Mossberg and Kara Swisher will ask it live on stage during D6.  The deadline is this Friday, May 23rd, and submission details and instructions are online at: http://allthingsd.com/d/ask-a-question/.

 

In addition to the luminaries mentioned above, we've added new speakers to the D6 lineup in the last few weeks, including Jerry Yang and Sue Decker of Yahoo, Sheryl Sandberg of Facebook and Rupert Murdoch of News Corporation. The full speaker list is available at: http://allthingsd.com/d/speakers/.

 

Best,
D6 Conference Team
